articles.title: Suriname advances toward a national early warning system that protects everyone, in time
articles.summary: Country: Suriname Source: UN Office for Disaster Risk Reduction Suriname is exposed to a range of hazards, including flooding, drought and wildfire, with heavy rains making flooding an increasingly frequent occurrence. While the country benefits from the absence of hurricanes and earthquakes, hazards associated with oil and gas extraction and with mining have affected communities directly. articles.title: Press Release: Government of South Sudan and United Nations reaffirm commitment to support 60,000 displaced people achieve sustainable solutions by end of 2026
articles.summary: Country: South Sudan Source: UN Resident and Humanitarian Coordinator in South Sudan Please refer to the attached file. Juba, 16 June 2026 – The Government of South Sudan and the United Nations (UN) reaffirmed their commitment to ending protracted displacement for at least 60,000 South Sudanese by the end of this year at the second Steering Committee Meeting on Durable Solutions in Juba. articles.title: France: MSF launches a new project in the Dunkirk area for migrant women
articles.summary: Countries: France, World Source: Médecins Sans Frontières Paris, 16 June 2026 – Near Dunkirk, more than a thousand displaced people are surviving in makeshift camps, with extremely limited access to healthcare. Among them is a growing number of isolated women who are vulnerable to violence. articles.title: Chinese Hackers Abused Google Workspace Rules to Steal Research and Defense Emails
articles.summary: A China-linked espionage group hid inside North American medical, academic, and military research networks for more than a year, quietly stealing sensitive research and defense email. The way in was a backdoor on their REDCap research servers that stole login credentials. The exfiltration was the unusual part: the attackers rewired the victims' own Google Workspace rules to copy any message

articles.summary: Bad actors are exploiting multiple security vulnerabilities in Fortinet FortiSandbox, according to threat intelligence firm Defused Cyber. articles.title: China-Linked SprySOCKS Backdoor Expands to Windows with Driver-Based Stealth
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged two previously undocumented Windows variants of what was believed to be a Linux-only backdoor called SprySOCKS. articles.summary: For many Iranians, the question is not whether the deal means victory, but whether it lowers prices and reduces fear of another war.

articles.title: Triple climate threats affect nearly half the world’s children

articles.title: Global fish production hits new high, but climate threats loom

articles.title: WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation
articles.summary: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) has praised Uganda’s response to an Ebola outbreak that has spread from neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while warning that continued vigilance and cross-border cooperation will be critical to stopping transmission.

articles.title: These new solid-state ACs promise a cool future. Scientists aren’t so sure.
articles.summary: After three years of record-­breaking heat, this one is set to be yet another scorcher. Air-conditioning? Not going anywhere. The International Energy Agency projects that the number of AC units will triple by 2050. That’s good for health—one Lancet study estimated that AC prevented nearly 200,000 premature deaths in 2019 alone—but bad for the planet.…

articles.title: New Rokarolla Android Malware Steals PINs, SMS Codes, and Crypto Wallet Funds
articles.summary: Security researchers at Zimperium's zLabs have documented a new Android banking trojan, Rokarolla, that targets 217 banking and cryptocurrency apps and packs 137 remote commands. Together, they give an operator near-total control of an infected phone: it lifts lock-screen PINs, reads and sends SMS, rewrites the clipboard to redirect crypto payments, and switches off Google Play
